summary: Vaswani et al. introduce the Transformer, a sequence transduction architecture based solely on attention mechanisms that dispenses with recurrence and convolutions, achieving state-of-the-art BLEU scores on WMT 2014 English-to-German (28.4) and English-to-French (41.8) translation while requiring significantly less training time.

blocks.content: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks in an encoder-decoder configuration.

source_span.quote: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks in an encoder-decoder configuration.

blocks.content: The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism.

source_span.quote: The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism.

blocks.content: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

source_span.quote: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

blocks.content: Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train.

source_span.quote: Experiments on two machine translation tasks show these models to be superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train.

blocks.content: Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, improving over the existing best results, including ensembles by over 2 BLEU.

source_span.quote: Our model achieves 28.4 BLEU on the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, improving over the existing best results, including ensembles by over 2 BLEU.

blocks.content: On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, our model establishes a new single-model state-of-the-art BLEU score of 41.8 after training for 3.5 days on eight GPUs, a small fraction of the training costs of the best models from the literature.

source_span.quote: On the WMT 2014 English-to-French translation task, our model establishes a new single-model state-of-the-art BLEU score of 41.8 after training for 3.5 days on eight GPUs, a small fraction of the training costs of the best models from the literature.

blocks.content: We show that the Transformer generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.

source_span.quote: We show that the Transformer generalizes well to other tasks by applying it successfully to English constituency parsing both with large and limited training data.
